content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of NNN positive integers, you have to print KKK elements such that summation of product between in each pair is minimised i.e ∑i=1K∑j=i+1KAi×Aj\sum_{i=1}^K \sum_{j=i+1}^K A_i \times A_j∑i=1K​∑j=i+1K​Ai​×Aj​ is minimum


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains 2 integer NNN - size of array AAA and KKK
Second line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,.....,ANA_1, A_2, ....., A_NA1​,A2​,.....,AN​

Sum of NNN over all test cases will not exceed 10510^5105



Output Format

For each testcase, output KKK space-separated integers in non-decreasing order.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains two integers N and K (1 ≤ K ≤ N). The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output K integers in non‑decreasing order – the K elements whose pairwise product sum is minimum. Print the K numbers on one line separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we sort the array and take the K smallest numbers.
Test 1: sorted [1,2,4] → first 2 → 1 2.
Test 2: only one element → 987.
Test 3: sorted [1,1,2,1000000000] → first 3 → 1 1 2.

solution.approach:
All numbers are positive. For any selected set S, the contribution of an element x to the total sum of pairwise products is x multiplied by the sum of the other (K‑1) chosen elements, which is always non‑negative. Replacing any chosen element by a larger one can only increase the total sum. Therefore the optimal set consists of the K smallest values of the array. The algorithm for each test case is:
1. Read N, K and the array.
2. Sort the array in non‑decreasing order.
3. Output the first K elements.
The sorting step dominates the complexity.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (sorting). The total over all test cases is O((ΣN) log Nmax), where ΣN ≤ 10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array of each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        arr.sort()
        out_lines.append(' '.join(str(x) for x in arr[:K]))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
